title: 2016-07-20 | SB 3.25.5 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: In the absence of the father, it is the duty of the grown son to take charge of his mother and serve her to the best of his ability so that she will not feel separation from her husband.
And it is the duty of the husband to leave home as soon as there is a grown son to take charge of his wife and family affairs.
That is the Vedic system of household life.
One should not remain continually implicated in household affairs after the time of death.
He must leave. Family affairs and wife may be taken charge of by a grown son.
So, half late we are hearing many things about mobiles and laptops and computers.
Every other day we see that a new mobile is released and a new laptop is released. It's like that.
So, one of the newspapers have reported that anybody who looks at the laptop screen continually for three to four hours, three to four hours every day,
they develop a disease called computer syndrome disease.
And one of the things that happens is because you are staring at the screen, people are not blinking their eyes.
They are not blinking. They continuously watch.

They are in the eye. You know, eyes, generally, they are a little watery.

And, you know, like that, there is a pain if you look at the screen.
